title: BITSAT 2020
question: The molar heat of vaporisation of water at 100$$^\circ$$C is 40.585 kJ/mol. The temperature at which a solution containing 5.60 g of Al2(SO4)3 per 1000 g of water boil is __________. (Assuming the degree of ionisation of salt to be 1).
solution:
$$\therefore$$ $${K_b} = {{RT_b^2{M_1}} \over {\Delta {H_{vap}}}} = {{8.314 \times {{(373)}^2} \times 0.0180} \over {40585}}$$ = 0.513 K kg/mol

Now, molecular weight of $$A{l_2}{(S{O_4})_3} = 342$$

$$\mathop {A{l_2}{{(S{O_4})}_3}}\limits_{1 - \alpha } $$ $$\rightleftharpoons$$ $$\mathop {2A{l^{3 + }}}\limits_{2\alpha } $$ + $$\mathop {3SO_2^{2 - }}\limits_{2\alpha } $$

i = 1 $$-$$ $$\alpha$$

+ 2$$\alpha$$ + 3$$\alpha$$ = 1 + 4$$\alpha$$ $$\Rightarrow$$ i = 5 ($$\because$$ $$\alpha$$ = 1)

$$\Delta$$Tb = i $$\times$$ Kb . m

$$ = 5 \times 0.513 \times {{5.6} \over {342 \times 1}} = 0.042$$

Boiling point of solution = 100 + 0.042 = 100.042$$^\circ$$C

title: BITSAT 2021
question: The relative lowering of vapour pressure of a dilute aqueous solution containing non-volatile solute is 0.0125. The molality of the solution is about
solution:
According to Raoult's law, relative lowering of vapour pressure is equal to mole fraction of solute,

$$\therefore$$ $${{p - {p_s}} \over p} = {n \over {n + N}} = {w \over m}{{w/m} \over {{w \over m} + {W \over M}}} \Rightarrow {w \over m} < < < {W \over M}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{p^\circ - {p_s}} \over {p^\circ }} = {{w/m} \over {{W \over M}}} = {w \over m} \times {M \over W}$$

$$0.0125 = {{w \times 18} \over {m \times W}} \Rightarrow {w \over {mW}} = {{0.0125} \over {18}}$$

$$ = {{Weight\,of\,solute\,(w) \times 1000} \over {Molar\,mass\,of\,solute\,(m) \times Weight\,of\,{H_2}O(W)}}$$

Now, molality $$m = {{0.0125} \over {18}} \times 1000 = 0.69 \approx 0.7$$

title: BITSAT 2024
question: 58.5 g of NaCl and 180 g of glucose were separately dissolved in 1000 mL of water. Identify the correct statement regarding the elevation of boiling point of the resulting solution.
options: NaCl solution will show higher elevation of boiling point.
Glucose solution will show higher elevation of boiling point.
Both the solution will show equal elevation of boiling point.
None will show boiling point elevation.
solution:
$ 58.5 \mathrm{~g} \mathrm{NaCl}=\frac{58.5}{58.5} \mathrm{~mol} $.

$ \begin{aligned} & =1 \mathrm{~mol} \\ 180 \mathrm{~g} \text { of glucose } & =\frac{180}{180}=1 \mathrm{~mol} \end{aligned} $

Thus, we have 1 M NaCl and 1 M glucose solution.

But NaCl dissociates in the solution and glucose does not dissociates.

Thus particle concentration is more in NaCl .

Hence, NaCl solution will have higher elevation in boiling point.

title: BITSAT 2024
question: A dimolar solution potassium ferrocyanide is $ 50 \% $ dissociated at 300 K . The osmotic pressure of solution is $ \left(R=8.314 \mathrm{JK}^{-1} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}\right) $
solution:
$ \alpha=0.5 $ (50\% dissociated)

The reaction involves is

$ \mathrm{K}_{4}\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right] \rightleftharpoons 4 \mathrm{~K}^{+}+\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]^{4} $

$\begin{array}{1} \text { Initial moles } & 1 & 0 & 0 \\ \text { Moles after dissociation } & 1-\alpha & 4 \alpha & \alpha \end{array}$

Total moles at equilibrium $ =1-\alpha+4 \alpha+\alpha $

Total $ =1+4 \alpha $

$ \therefore $ van't Hoff factor $ (i)=\frac{1+4 \alpha}{1}=1+4 \times 0.5=3 $

Osmotic pressure, $ \pi=i C R T $

$ \begin{aligned} C & =0.1 \mathrm{M}=10^{2} \mathrm{~mol} \mathrm{~m}^{-3} \\ \pi & =3 \times 10^{2} \times 8.314 \times 300 \\ & =7.483 \times 10^{5} \mathrm{Nm}^{-2} \end{aligned} $

or $ \quad \pi=7.48 \mathrm{~atm} $

title: BITSAT 2025
question: A 1 molar aqueous solution of magnesium nitrate is $40 \%$ dissociated at 298 K . Calculate the osmotic pressure of the solution. $\left(R=0.0821 \mathrm{~L} \mathrm{~atm} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}\right)$
solution:
Magnesium nitrate separates into ions in solution as shown below:

$$ \mathrm{Mg}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{Mg}^{2+} + 2\mathrm{NO}_3^{-} $$

This means that one unit of magnesium nitrate produces a total of 3 particles after complete dissociation (1 magnesium ion and 2 nitrate ions).

If the salt is only 40% dissociated, it means $\alpha = 0.4$.

The Van’t Hoff factor, $ i $, which shows how many times the number of effective particles increases due to dissociation, is given by:

$$ i = 1 + \alpha (n - 1) $$

Substituting the values, we get:

$$ i = 1 + 0.4(3 - 1) = 1 + 0.8 = 1.8 $$

title: BITSAT 2025
question: 0.1 m of urea and 0.05 m of $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ are dissolved separately in equal volumes of water. Which solution will have higher elevation in boiling point?
options:
Urea solution

$\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ solution

Both will show equal elevation

None will show elevation
solution:
Elevation of boiling point depends on the vant' Hoff factor (i).

Urea is non-electrolyte, $i=1$

$\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ dissociates into 3 ions, $i=3$

So, effective particles $=0.05 \times 3=0.15 \mathrm{~mol}$ for $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ is more than 0.1 mol for urea is equal amount of solvent.

Hence, $\mathrm{CaCl}_2$ solution will show greater elevation.
